title: FEMA climate whitewash — $115 billion in climate damage, one word in 150 pages — 2026-05-17
narrative: Trump-appointed Review Council released a FEMA overhaul proposal, framed as modernization and efficiency improvement. Administration officials say the changes streamline disaster response and reduce bureaucratic friction.
telemetry: — The 150+ page FEMA overhaul report contains exactly ONE mention of the word 'climate' — buried, not central
— 2025 was the third-highest year for billion-dollar climate disasters in US history
— 23 separate billion-dollar climate events in 2025; 276 deaths; $115 billion in total damages
— FEMA was architecturally designed around climate-adjacent disaster response — hurricanes, floods, wildfires are all climate-amplified
— Any credible overhaul of a disaster agency that managed $115B in climate damages must address climate as a core variable — not a footnote
— The administration simultaneously withdrew from the Paris Agreement and is cutting climate agency capacity while this review was conducted
analysis:
An agency that processed $115 billion in climate disaster costs in a single year was reviewed by a council that could not bring itself to name the cause. One mention of 'climate' in 150 pages is not a gap — it's an institutional decision.

The review council was appointed by the same administration that withdrew from the Paris Agreement. The absence of climate language is not an oversight. It is the conclusion.

Headline: 'FEMA Got $115 Billion in Climate Damage Last Year. The Official Reform Report Says Climate Isn't the Problem.'

• — The 150+ page FEMA overhaul report contains exactly ONE mention of the word 'climate' — buried, not central
• — 2025 was the third-highest year for billion-dollar climate disasters in US history
• — 23 separate billion-dollar climate events in 2025; 276 deaths; $115 billion in total damages

The death count — 276 — is a number that fits inside a single sentence. The report's authors knew it was there. They chose not to build a sentence around it.
verdict:
[SIPHONED VERDICT]: Headline: 'FEMA Got $115 Billion in Climate Damage Last Year. The Official Reform Report Says Climate Isn't the Problem.'

An agency that processed $115 billion in climate disaster costs in a single year was reviewed by a panel that could not bring itself to name the cause. One mention of 'climate' in 150 pages is not a gap — it is the conclusion. The 276 dead from 2025 are not unnamed in the report by accident. The death count was known. A decision was made. The reform framework that cannot name climate as a variable cannot fix the system that climate is breaking.
